content: Next.js App Router architecture for external API proxying: Route Handlers (app/api/*/route.ts) run on Node.js server, can read process.env server secrets. Pattern: client calls /api/places/details?placeId=X, route handler calls Google Places API with secret key, caches response (Redis or in-memory), returns to client. Can also use React Server Components to fetch data server-side directly. For caching, use Next.js built-in fetch cache or Redis. Route handlers support all HTTP methods and can implement rate limiting per user.
